metadata.title: Breeder profile web research - July 15 batch 6 G
metadata.notes: Public-web research only. Historical and current affiliations were separated from legal title, and no later employer was treated as a rightsholder without assignment evidence. Public-institution and corporate employees without personal title are marked not_actionable_verified. No external communication was made.

profiles.ownershipType: public-university grape breeder and research director
profiles.ownershipSummary: The Hungarian plant-variety gazette names the University of Pecs as applicant and Peter Teszlak as breeder or co-breeder on the cited grape applications. PTE identifies him as a research leader. No source reviewed supports personal ownership by Teszlak.
profiles.leadershipSummary: The University of Pecs viticulture program has institutional continuity: Zoltan Madaras is institute president, Peter Teszlak is research director, and the institute also identifies a chief winemaker and laboratory team.

webResearchSources.label: Hungarian IP Office plant-variety gazette - University of Pecs applicant and breeder listings
University of Pecs - Peter Teszlak project profile and business contact
University of Pecs - institute leadership and research organization

profiles.webResearchNotes: Exact identity resolved to Dr. Peter Teszlak of the University of Pecs. Cultivar evidence is tied to University applications; the individual should not be scored as an independent owner. Headquarters: Pecs, Hungary. Public business contact: teszlak.peter@pte.hu.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Japan's official plant-variety record for Koiminori names NARO as titleholder and Emi Kitadani as breeder. A 2025 paper continues to identify her with NARO's Kyushu Okinawa Agricultural Research Center. No personal title was found.
profiles.leadershipSummary: The cited varieties arise from a multi-person NARO strawberry program, providing institutional breeding and licensing continuity beyond any one breeder.

webResearchSources.label: MAFF Koiminori title record - NARO holder and Emi Kitadani breeder
NARO official Koiminori cultivar page
2025 Horticultural Research paper - current NARO affiliation

profiles.webResearchNotes: The exact name and institutional role are resolved. The legal title shown by the official register is NARO's, not Kitadani's personally. Headquarters: Tsukuba, Ibaraki, Japan. Public institutional contact: joint_research@naro.affrc.go.jp.

profiles.ownershipType: corporate stone-fruit co-breeder with patent-specific assignee chains
profiles.ownershipSummary: Wawona Packing was the original assignee on Wapeachone and related Wawona-origin patents. Recorded 2025 transactions shown in the patent record pass interests through Sun Valley Packing to Threefold Holdings Group, while the 2026 Threefoldpeachone patent directly names Threefold as assignee. Roberts' current Arable Advisory Group role is an employment affiliation, not evidence that Arable owns those rights.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Arable identifies John Slaughter as lead plant breeder and Kaylan Roberts as associate plant breeder, giving the current breeding team a visible two-person bench. Ownership still requires patent-by-patent chain review.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Arable's official team page supports Roberts' current employment, but no reviewed assignment establishes Arable as owner of the Wawona- or Threefold-assigned rights.

webResearchSources.label: Arable Advisory Group official team page - current Roberts and Slaughter roles
Wapeachone patent - inventors, original assignee and recorded assignment chain
USPTO Threefoldpeachone patent grant - Threefold assignee

profiles.webResearchNotes: Exact identity resolved. This is not an independently owned breeder profile: Roberts is a named co-inventor and corporate employee, and title must remain split by the recorded assignee for each patent. Suppress individual-owner scoring. Current operating geography: Fresno and Reedley, California, United States; use Arable's official team page for public business contact.

profiles.ownershipType: independent private breeder, nursery and plant-rights holder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Hortplant Jerzy Dominikowski is the operating sole-trader nursery and breeding business. Its official site presents an in-house strawberry breeding program, and the Hossa variety page identifies HORTPLANT Jerzy Dominikowski as the rights holder. Separately licensed public-institute varieties should not be treated as Hortplant-owned.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Jerzy Dominikowski is the named proprietor. The official site describes more than 40 years of operation and production above 10 million seedlings annually, but no named legal successor or management succession plan was found; founder continuity remains a diligence point.

webResearchSources.label: Hortplant official site - own breeding program, cultivars and contact
Hortplant privacy notice - legal operator and address
Hossa commercial variety record - Hortplant identified as rights holder

profiles.webResearchNotes: The exact entity name is the business identity, not merely a person's unstructured name. Verify the current PBR register and any encumbrances for each H-series cultivar during transaction diligence. Headquarters: Lisowola 12, 96-330 Puszcza Marianska, Poland. Public business contact: dominikowscy@hortplant.pl; +48 46 831 06 77.

profiles.ownershipType: former public-program breeder and current private-company founder/breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: The historical Sweet Jasper patent and official Israeli VARO records place title with the State of Israel/Agricultural Research Organization, despite naming Hanan Bazak as breeder. The later Big Bear patent instead names Bazak Agro Innovation Ltd as assignee. Historical ARO rights must not be reassigned to Bazak Agro merely because Bazak later founded or joined the private company.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Bazak Agro appears founder-dependent around Hanan Bazak, with no named succession bench found in the reviewed public sources. The historical ARO portfolio has government-institutional continuity and technology-transfer support.

webResearchSources.label: Sweet Jasper patent - State/ARO assignment and Hanan Bazak inventor
Israel Plant Breeders' Rights Gazette 92 - VARO holder and breeder codes
USPTO Big Bear grant - Bazak Agro Innovation assignee

profiles.webResearchNotes: Record split is mandatory. Score current private-company opportunities only against Bazak Agro-assigned cultivars; retain ARO/State title for the older public-program portfolio. Headquarters: Carmei Yosef, Israel for Bazak Agro; Rishon LeZion, Israel for the ARO legacy. Public business contact is limited to the official patent/PBR records linked here.

profiles.ownershipType: public-university viticulture leader and grape co-breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: The Hungarian plant-variety gazette names the University of Pecs as applicant and Zoltan Madaras as breeder or co-breeder on the cited grape applications. University sources identify Madaras as institute president and project professional leader. No personal ownership is supported.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Madaras leads the University of Pecs viticulture institute, with Peter Teszlak as research director and a broader winemaking and laboratory team providing institutional continuity.

webResearchSources.label: Hungarian IP Office plant-variety gazette - University of Pecs applicant and breeder listings
University of Pecs - Zoltan Madaras project profile and contact
University of Pecs - current institute leadership

profiles.webResearchNotes: Exact identity resolved to the University of Pecs institute leader. The University applicant record controls; Madaras is not an independent rightsholder. Headquarters: Pecs, Hungary. Public business contact: madaras.zoltan@pte.hu.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Official MAFF records for Harehime and Asumi name NARO as titleholder and Mutsuko Nonomura as a breeder. The Canadian Rinoka record likewise names NARO as applicant/holder. No personal title is supported.
profiles.leadershipSummary: The citrus releases are products of a multi-person NARO program, including Fumitaka Takishita and other institutional breeders, so the portfolio has organizational rather than individual continuity.

webResearchSources.label: MAFF Harehime title record - NARO holder and Nonomura breeder
MAFF Asumi title record - NARO holder and Nonomura breeder
NARO repository identity record - Mutsuko Nonomura/Nakano name history

profiles.webResearchNotes: NARO's repository supports a name-history link to Mutsuko Nakano. The breeder identity is resolved, but no current private affiliation or personal title was found. Headquarters: Tsukuba, Ibaraki, Japan. Public institutional contact: joint_research@naro.affrc.go.jp.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Official MAFF records for Harehime and Asumi name NARO as titleholder and Fumitaka Takishita as a breeder. The Canadian Rinoka record also names NARO as applicant/holder. A later university affiliation in research literature does not transfer these NARO rights.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Takishita worked within NARO's multi-person citrus breeding program. NARO's institutional licensing and research structure provides the relevant continuity rather than an individual succession path.

webResearchSources.label: MAFF Harehime title record - NARO holder and Takishita breeder
MAFF Asumi title record - NARO holder and Takishita breeder
Research paper - NARO citrus affiliation

profiles.webResearchNotes: Exact identity resolved to the NARO citrus breeder. Treat NARO as titleholder for the cited cultivars and do not infer title from later educational or research affiliations. Headquarters: Tsukuba, Ibaraki, Japan; the cited citrus work was in Shizuoka. Public institutional contact: joint_research@naro.affrc.go.jp.

profiles.ownershipSummary: The Tamir and Barak patent records identify Nir Dai as inventor and the State of Israel, through the Agricultural Research Organization, as assignee. Dai is publicly identified as an ARO/Volcani researcher. No personal ownership is supported.
profiles.leadershipSummary: ARO's strawberry work is an institutional breeding program, and Kidum is the dedicated unit for protecting and commercializing ARO intellectual property. Continuity therefore sits with the public organization and its team.

webResearchSources.label: Tamir patent - Nir Dai inventor and State of Israel assignee
Barak patent - State of Israel assignee
ARO official Kidum technology-transfer contact

profiles.webResearchNotes: The individual is a public-research employee and named breeder, not the personal rightsholder. Licensing or acquisition questions belong with ARO/Kidum. Headquarters: 68 HaMakabim Street, Rishon LeZion, Israel. Public business contact: kidum@agri.gov.il; +972-3-9683298.

profiles.ownershipSummary: The exact identity is M. Andrew Walker (Michael Andrew Walker), the UC Davis grape geneticist. The Camminare Noir patent names him as inventor and the Regents of the University of California as assignee. His emeritus status does not move title to him personally.
profiles.leadershipSummary: UC Davis reports that Luis Diaz-Garcia succeeded Walker and is restructuring the grape breeding program, providing a named institutional successor and active program continuity.

webResearchSources.label: UC Davis official Andrew Walker profile and contact
Camminare Noir patent - Regents of the University of California assignee
UC Davis - Luis Diaz-Garcia succession to the grape breeding program

profiles.webResearchNotes: Normalize the source name Andrew Walker to M. Andrew Walker/Michael Andrew Walker. The Regents, not the emeritus breeder or successor, hold the cited patent rights. Headquarters: Davis, California, United States. Public university contact: awalker@ucdavis.edu; +1 530-752-0902.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Lorenzo Burgos is a CEBAS-CSIC researcher and project leader in the public institute's stone-fruit breeding program. Official CSIC materials associate the cultivars and plant assets with CEBAS-CSIC; the reviewed sources do not support personal title by Burgos.
profiles.leadershipSummary: The program has an institutional bench. Current CEBAS project pages identify later leadership and participants including David Ruiz Gonzalez and Juan Alfonso Salazar Martinez, reducing single-breeder succession dependence.

webResearchSources.label: CEBAS-CSIC official institutional site
CSIC institute profile - CEBAS organization and protected plant outputs
CSIC journal profile - CEBAS affiliation and public business email

profiles.webResearchNotes: Exact identity resolved to the Murcia-based CEBAS-CSIC researcher. Treat the public institute as the rights counterparty and verify title cultivar by cultivar in CPVO or the applicable national register. Headquarters: Campus Universitario de Espinardo, 30100 Murcia, Spain. Public business contact: burgos@cebas.csic.es; +34 968 396 200.

profiles.ownershipSummary: The exact identity is Terence Lee Robinson, Cornell professor at Cornell AgriTech. The G.969 patent names Robinson as one of several inventors and Cornell University as assignee. No personal title is supported.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Cornell's Geneva rootstock program has a documented multi-person bench, including Robinson, Gennaro Fazio and other Cornell/USDA contributors, with institutional licensing through Cornell.

webResearchSources.label: Cornell official Terence Robinson profile and contact
G.969 patent - Cornell University assignee and Robinson co-inventor
Cornell technology licensing catalog - rootstocks

profiles.webResearchNotes: Normalize the source order Robinson Terence Lee to Terence Lee Robinson. Cornell is the cited assignee and licensing counterparty; suppress personal-owner scoring. Headquarters: Cornell AgriTech, Geneva, New York, United States. Public university contact: tlr1@cornell.edu; +1 315-787-2227.

profiles.ownershipType: private family breeding group with a dedicated IP-holding company
profiles.ownershipSummary: Benoit Escande Editions is the documented breeder/IP holder for cited Escande varieties, while SCEA Pepinieres et Vergers Escande is the operating nursery and variety-maintenance company. The official site identifies Benoit Escande as director and third-generation leader. Rights should remain separated between the IP company and operating company according to each record.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Benoit Escande is the third-generation owner-leader. The group publishes a functional bench across technical, commercial and administrative roles, but no named next-generation legal successor was found; ownership succession remains a diligence item.

webResearchSources.label: Escande official site - family history and integrated breeding group
French official journal - Benoit Escande Editions breeder and SCEA maintainer
EU trademark record - live ESCANDE mark owned by Benoit Escande Editions

profiles.webResearchNotes: This is an actionable private breeding platform. Diligence should confirm the EURL ownership/capital structure, title for each cultivar, and which portfolio varieties are owned versus represented under license. Headquarters: Millet, 47500 Saint-Vite, France. Public business contact: info@pepinieres-escande.com; +33 5 53 71 22 13. The live ESCANDE mark is supported by the linked public trademark record; cultivar names are not asserted here as trademarks.

profiles.ownershipType: retired public-research grape breeder and later private consultant
profiles.ownershipSummary: Embrapa identifies Umberto Camargo as the retired coordinator of its national grape breeding program and credits the program's BRS cultivar releases to institutional work. Brazilian official protection records name him as breeder, not personal owner. His later Vino Vitis consulting affiliation does not transfer historical Embrapa rights.
profiles.leadershipSummary: The Embrapa program continued after Camargo's 2009 retirement with a multi-person team, including Patricia Ritschel and Joao Dimas Garcia Maia in later releases, providing clear institutional succession.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: An Embrapa-hosted publication supports Camargo's post-retirement consulting affiliation and business email, but no assignment evidence makes Vino Vitis the owner of historical Embrapa BRS rights.

webResearchSources.label: Embrapa official profile - Camargo retirement, program leadership and cultivar output
Brazilian Ministry cultivar protection record - BRS Carmem breeder record
Embrapa-hosted publication - later Vino Vitis consulting affiliation

profiles.webResearchNotes: The source name resolves to the retired Embrapa program leader. Keep all historical BRS title with the registered institutional owner unless a specific assignment proves otherwise; Vino Vitis is candidate affiliation only. Current geography: Bento Goncalves, Rio Grande do Sul, Brazil. Public consulting contact: umberto.camargo@gmail.com.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Manuel Rubio Angulo is identified by CEBAS-CSIC as a project principal investigator and participant in its public stone-fruit breeding program. The institute, not the individual researcher, is the supported rights counterparty for the cited CEBAS portfolio.
profiles.leadershipSummary: CEBAS project pages show continuity from Rubio's earlier principal-investigator role to current leaders David Ruiz Gonzalez and Juan Alfonso Salazar Martinez, backed by a broader breeding and biotechnology team.

webResearchSources.label: CEBAS-CSIC official institutional site
CSIC institute profile - CEBAS organization and protected plant outputs
CSIC journal paper - Manuel Rubio's CEBAS-CSIC affiliation

profiles.webResearchNotes: Exact identity resolved to the CEBAS-CSIC researcher. This is a public-institution profile without supported personal title; verify each cultivar's current holder in CPVO or the applicable national register. Headquarters: Campus Universitario de Espinardo, 30100 Murcia, Spain. Public institute contact: +34 968 396 200 via the linked CEBAS group page.
